hypothesis: If crystal material property prediction models integrate complementary information from multiple modalities (graph structure, natural language descriptions, and X-ray diffraction spectra) through cross-modal predictive learning in a shared latent space, then they will achieve lower mean absolute error on benchmark crystal property prediction tasks compared to single-modality baseline models such as CGCNN, CoGN, and ALIGNN, because each modality captures distinct physicochemical features that together provide a more comprehensive representation of material properties than any single modality alone.
